# Next.js Documentation

> Next.js is a React framework for building full-stack web applications. It provides file-based routing, server-side rendering, static site generation, and API routes out of the box.

## Docs

- [Introduction](https://docs.example.com/documentation/index): Overview, project structure, quick example
- [Installation](https://docs.example.com/documentation/installation): Create a project, system requirements, troubleshooting
- [Quickstart](https://docs.example.com/documentation/quickstart): Build pages, fetch data, add styling

## Routing

- [Pages and Layouts](https://docs.example.com/documentation/routing/pages-and-layouts): File-based routing, nested layouts, route groups
- [Dynamic Routes](https://docs.example.com/documentation/routing/dynamic-routes): Dynamic segments, catch-all routes, generateStaticParams
- [Middleware](https://docs.example.com/documentation/routing/middleware): Auth, redirects, headers, geolocation

## Data Fetching

- [Overview](https://docs.example.com/documentation/data-fetching/overview): Server vs client rendering, composing components
- [Caching](https://docs.example.com/documentation/data-fetching/caching): Data cache, revalidation, route segment options

## Styling

- [Overview](https://docs.example.com/documentation/styling/overview): CSS Modules, Tailwind, global CSS, CSS-in-JS

## API Reference

- [Route Handlers](https://docs.example.com/api-reference/api/route-handlers): API endpoints, HTTP methods, dynamic routes
- [Configuration](https://docs.example.com/api-reference/api/configuration): next.config.ts options, env variables, TypeScript

## Deployment

- [Overview](https://docs.example.com/deployment/deployment/overview): Vercel, Docker, static export, standalone
- [Docker](https://docs.example.com/deployment/deployment/docker): Dockerfile, docker-compose, health checks, optimization
